asteria_policy_reference.baseline_contract: Apply the established Asteria contact canonicalization, identity, field-provenance, contested-identifier, and outreach-readiness controls inferred from the solved train examples. This case scope supplies task-specific populations and reporting contracts; it does not restate those transferable controls.

snapshot_admission_replay_contract.scope: Final baseline owners containing at least one row in the inclusive lexical range WAR-C00001 through WAR-C00140; include every final member row of each such owner.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.baseline_reference: Final owner membership, final master-ID labels, and the final contested-phone set remain fixed throughout this task-specific replay.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.initial_state: No scoped row has been admitted.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.event_order: Within each stage, order rows by business_updated_at ascending and then row_id ascending lexicographically.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.event_state: For an admitted row, before state is its fixed final owner's canonical state over the earlier admitted rows of that owner; after state is the canonical state over that prefix plus the current row. Apply the established Asteria policies to each state. Null before-state fields mean that the owner was not yet present.

effect_definitions.DISCOVERED: No before state exists; changed_canonical_fields is empty.
effect_definitions.CANONICAL_STATE_CHANGED: A before state exists and its selected row, at least one canonical impact field, or readiness disposition differs after admission.
effect_definitions.NONSELECTED: A before state exists and those reported state elements do not differ after admission.

snapshot_admission_replay_contract.running_count_contract: running_present_owner_count and running_ready_owner_count are the scoped post-event counts; ready includes READY and READY_WITH_CHANNEL_RESTRICTION.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.control_contract: For every existing before or after state, report the established Asteria field-provenance and outreach codes for that state; use JSON null when no state exists.
snapshot_admission_replay_contract.output_order: Return every scoped row event in the defined processing order and stage summaries in stage_sequence order.

unsafe_identity_rule_impact_contract.baseline_population: All final baseline canonical owners, labelled by final master_id.

independent_counterfactual_partitions.group_key: the owner's non-null canonical_phone_digits, including values restricted by the established Asteria contested-identifier control
the owner's established-Asteria-normalized canonical_name together with canonical_region_code

unsafe_identity_rule_impact_contract.partition_semantics: Evaluate each policy independently. Owners sharing its non-null group key form one counterfactual owner; all other owners remain singleton counterfactual owners. A group with more than one baseline owner is a multi-owner component, and each unordered pair within it is a false pair.

aggregate_definitions.resulting_owner_count: the number of counterfactual owners in the independent partition
aggregate_definitions.multi_owner_component_count: components containing at least two baseline owners
aggregate_definitions.false_pair_count: the sum of unordered baseline-owner pairs within multi-owner components
aggregate_definitions.cross_region_false_pair_count: false pairs whose canonical_region_code values differ

component_reporting.component_order: Within a policy, sort by owner count descending and then by the lexicographically sorted master-ID array ascending; assign C001 onward.
component_reporting.largest_component_tie_break: Greatest owner count, then lexicographically smallest sorted master-ID array.
component_reporting.histogram_order: Include only sizes greater than one, ordered by component_size ascending.
component_reporting.control_contract: Apply the established Asteria shared/recycled-identifier identity control to ANY_SHARED_PHONE and the established name-only/noncorroborating identity control to both name policies. Select the matching controlled disposition from the answer contract.

unsafe_household_outreach_contract.baseline_population: All final canonical owners, labelled by final master_id.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.household_partition: Group owners by the final surname token of canonical_name under established Asteria name normalization. Every group is a household component, including groups without a release anchor.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.release_anchor: An owner with at least one final member row whose consent_status is GRANTED under established Asteria value normalization.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.unsafe_release: A household having an anchor releases each member through that member's own email, or otherwise its own phone when that phone is not in the fixed final contested-phone set. This counterfactual ignores the member's final canonical consent and record status. Owners without such a route are not released.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.wrongful_release: An unsafe release whose correct final readiness is neither READY nor READY_WITH_CHANNEL_RESTRICTION.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.wrongful_risk_units: For one wrongful release: 1, plus the count of anchors in its household with a different canonical region, plus 4 when its correct readiness is BLOCKED_INACTIVE.

remediation_actions.REVIEW_ALL_ANCHORS: REVIEW:<component_id> prevents all releases from that component; cost is the sum over its anchors of 1 + floor((final member-row count - 1) / 2).
remediation_actions.BLOCK_WRONGFUL_OWNER: BLOCK:<baseline_master_id> prevents only that wrongful owner; cost is 2.
remediation_actions.mutual_exclusion: For one component, select REVIEW_ALL_ANCHORS or any subset of BLOCK_WRONGFUL_OWNER actions, never both.

objective_order: maximize total prevented wrongful risk units
choose the lexicographically smallest sorted action-key array

unsafe_household_outreach_contract.component_order: Owner count descending, then sorted master-ID array ascending; assign SURNAME_HOUSEHOLD-C001 onward.
unsafe_household_outreach_contract.required_evidence: Return the published budget, only the sorted optimal action-key objects, and the complete sorted prevented_wrongful_master_ids set.

source_removal_stability_contract.baseline_reference: Keep final owner membership, final master-ID labels, and the final contested-phone set fixed.

designated_outage.state_definition: For each fixed owner, the outage state is its canonical state under established Asteria policies using only member rows outside the removed sources.

source_removal_stability_contract.restoration_candidate: For an owner whose baseline selected row belongs to a removed source, restore only that row to the outage state. It is a candidate when selected row, all canonical impact fields, and readiness exactly equal the full baseline state.
source_removal_stability_contract.candidate_cost_units: Warranty Claims costs 2 and Identity Registry costs 3, plus 1 when the restored row is not verified.

objective_order: maximize selected owners whose baseline readiness is READY or READY_WITH_CHANNEL_RESTRICTION
choose the lexicographically smallest sorted restored-row-ID array

source_removal_stability_contract.required_evidence: Return the outage ID, published budget, every selected action with the answer-contract evidence fields, and the complete sorted recovered_master_ids set.

contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.business_goal: Derive the complete review population and decide whether proposed donor evidence belongs to the target's final canonical owner. Apply established Asteria identity and outreach controls; never carry another owner's channel into the target's release decision.

candidate_families.INTRA: Every final owner with more than one member row. target_row_id is its lexicographically smallest member; proposed_donor_row_id is its final master ID; candidate_id is TRANSFER-INTRA-<master_id>.
candidate_families.EXCEPTION: Every single-row final owner whose canonical phone is in the fixed final contested-phone set or whose final readiness is BLOCKED_NO_CONTACT or BLOCKED_INACTIVE, when the donor selection contract yields another owner. target_row_id is its master ID; candidate_id is TRANSFER-EXCEPTION-<master_id>.
candidate_families.exception_donor_selection: For a target bearing a contested canonical phone, select the lexicographically smallest different final master bearing that phone. Otherwise select the lexicographically smallest different final master having the same established-Asteria-normalized canonical name.

contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.resolution_evidence: Resolve target and donor independently under the established Asteria baseline policy and return both complete final memberships and stable master IDs.
contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.relationship_evidence: Report every normalized identifier shared by the two anchor rows in NAME, EMAIL, PHONE order, and select the applicable controlled relation_class under established Asteria identity policy.
contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.target_evidence: Return the target owner's final canonical channels, fixed contested-phone restriction, consent, record status, and readiness. Preserve JSON null for absent canonical channels.
contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.decision_evidence: Return the controlled transfer permission, adjudication action, and escalation flag consistent with the resolved relationship and target's final readiness under established Asteria controls.
contact_transfer_adjudication_contract.candidate_order: Return all INTRA candidates by target_owner_master_id ascending, then all EXCEPTION candidates by target_owner_master_id ascending. Sort every row-ID array lexicographically.
